revision.text:
{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |}

{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |} [[Category:Templates]]

{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |}

{{view|Template:Resize/doc/main|edit|brackets=yes}} It is to change the font size of a text string.
== Usage == * {{tc|resize|''N''|''text''}} — ''N'' specifies a size, such as "95%", "1.2em", etc. * {{tc|resize|''text''}} — If ''N'' is omitted, as in the second line above, a default 90% size is applied. * {{tc|resize|2=div=yes|3=''text''}} — As above but uses a div tag instead of a span tag. === Beware === '''{{red|Beware}}''' that if ''text'' happens to contain an equality sign (=), it will fail to render properly. Here are the workarounds: # Replace '''all''' instances of = with {{tlx|{{=}}}}, or # Specify {{tlf|resize|''N''|'''2='''''text''}} or {{tlf|resize|'''1='''''text''}}. Also it will fail to wrap multiple blocks, such as multiple paragraphs or multiple bullet points. Here are the workarounds: # Use, for example,
<div style="font-size:90%;">
...''multiple paragraphs''...
</div>, or # Apply this template separately to each block of paragraph, bullet point etc. # Use {{tlf|resize|2=div=yes}}. # Use {{tl|smalldiv}}. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" |- ! Code !! Result |- | {{tc|resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} || {{resize|This text defaults to 90% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} || {{resize|80%|This text is 80% normal size.}} |- | {{tc|resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} || {{resize|1.2em|This text is 0.2em larger than normal.}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} || {{resize|We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. It will fail to render}} |- | {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} || {{resize|We know 1+1{{=}}2 and 2+2{{=}}4.}} |- | {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} || {{resize|1=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4.}} |- | {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} || {{resize|120%|2=We know 1+1=2 and 2+2=4. This text is 120% normal size.}} |}
